title: enrich: full-corpus A/B glossary vs no-glossary to validate dropping glossary field
description:
## Why

7-doc smoke (sample/baseline vs hybrid swap) showed no-glossary enrichment is ≥ baseline retrieval on 7/7 queries, with 1/7 strict improvement (Q4 \"signer details with INN tax id\" → ExtendedSignerDetails rose from #2 to #1, displacing generation.md).

Inline bilingual mentions in summary (\`Inn (tax identification number)\`, \`ТОРГ-12 (goods consignment note)\`) embed at least as well as YAML \`glossary: {Inn: ИНН ...}\` for cross-lingual retrieval. Cost savings: ~50% smaller LLM output, eliminates docf-5oj (glossary direction bug) at the source.

But n=7 is thin. Need full-corpus rerun before committing schema change.

## What

Decision experiment:

1. Re-enrich all 642 diadok docs with no-glossary prompt (probe at /tmp/enrich-no-glossary.mjs is the prototype — promote to a flag in docforge enrich)
2. Index + embed in dedicated qmd collection \`diadok-md-noglossary-642\` (vectors auto-deduped if hashes overlap with diadok-md, but content differs so expect 2600+ new embeddings)
3. Run the full 8-query original retrieval smoke (Q1-Q8 from 2026-05-10 session) PLUS the 7 targeted queries (Q9-Q15 from this session) with rerank ENABLED (not --no-rerank)
4. Score per-query: top-1 doc match (binary), top-3 doc match (binary), MRR contribution
5. Aggregate: % queries where no-glossary top-1 = baseline top-1 (no-regression rate); % queries where no-glossary top-1 better; % worse

## Acceptance criteria for dropping glossary

- No-regression rate ≥ 90% (out of ~15 queries, ≤ 1 may regress)
- No-glossary improvement count ≥ baseline-improvement count
- Wall-clock per doc: no-glossary ≤ baseline (sanity; should be faster)
- Eyeball spot-check: bilingual term coverage in 5 random no-glossary summaries → all major Russian terms have EN equivalents in parens

## Decision

If criteria met → proceed to schema-v2 bead (drop glossary field, depend on this).
If not met → keep glossary, address docf-5oj separately.

## Notes

- Use 9B model for both (fair comparison; 4B already shown worse in earlier session)
- Concurrency=1, parallel=1 for stable timing
- Embedding model: qwen3-embedding-8b (already canonical via QMD_EMBED_MODEL env, just mislabeled in DB metadata)
- Time budget: ~15 min enrich + 5 min embed + 5 min query = 25 min total
notes:
VERDICT 2026-05-11: gold-judged scoring PASS. 3-way A/B against manually defined gold (B/N/T):
- top1@gold: baseline 10/15, nogloss 13/15 (winner), tight 8/15
- top3@gold: baseline 13/15, nogloss 15/15 (perfect), tight 11/15
- MRR: 0.789 / 0.933 / 0.677

NoGloss vs baseline pairwise: 4 wins, 10 ties, 1 regression (Q7 'electronic signature concept' → picks glossary/index.md instead of glossary/signature.md; signature.md still in top-3).

Spec acceptance criteria PASS:
- no-regression rate 14/15 = 93.3% > 90%
- improvement count (4) > regression count (1)

Tight prompt (extra 'no enumeration' instructions) HURT — suppressed useful inline bilingual mentions. 8/15 worst score.

Closing PASS. Proceed to docf-eew (schema v2 ship).

Artifacts:
- score script: /tmp/score-gold.mjs
- raw traces: /tmp/score-gold.json
- prior 3-way (file-match): /tmp/score-3way.tsv
- enrich runners: /tmp/enrich-no-glossary-642.mjs (nogloss), /tmp/enrich-tight-642.mjs (tight)
- collections: diadok-md-noglossary-642 (kept for posterity), diadok-md-tight-642 (kept)
close_reason: Closed

title: Save images to Obsidian vault (--save-images, format=obsidian)
description: docforge --format obsidian keeps ![](src) refs but never writes image bytes (output.ts:22-25) → broken embeds in Obsidian. Add --save-images flag: copy referenced rasters (png/jpg/webp/gif/bmp + data:) into <out>/_assets/<sha16>.<ext> and rewrite refs to ![[hash.ext]] Obsidian embeds. Both URL (fetch) and local (file:// read) sources. New src/assets/ module (store/core/index) mirroring src/vlm/. CLI only; MCP out of scope (URL-only + listPages pollution). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-obsidian-image-assets-design.md
close_reason: --save-images obsidian sidecar assets implemented + tested (8 tasks TDD, 445 tests, full review)

title: URL-crawl internal links not relativized → no obsidian wikilinks, default points to live site
description:
When converting a URL source (site crawl), same-origin internal links are left as absolute live URLs (e.g. https://host/a/b) instead of being rewritten to point at the converted local copies. Consequences:
- --format obsidian: produces ZERO [[wikilinks]] for crawled sites (toObsidianWikilinks correctly skips https:// targets). The headline obsidian feature is effectively inert for the common case (crawled doc sites).
- --format default: internal links point at the LIVE site, not the local .md corpus — wrong for an offline/RAG corpus, which should be self-contained.

OBSERVED (live test 2026-05-24): crawled https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/ (MkDocs/Material) → --format obsidian. Frontmatter correct on all pages; 0 wikilinks; 500+ links remained absolute https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/.... The existing default-format beads corpus (qmd beads-docs, /home/igi21/experiements/beads/docforge-output/beads) likewise has 524 absolute https links.

ROOT CAUSE: For URL sources the pipeline never relativizes same-origin links. src/links.ts rewriteInternalLinks only handles RELATIVE .html→.md (skips https). No step maps absolute same-origin links to the corpus-relative .md output path. (Contrast: docf-7w5 fixed LOCAL-dir relative links via synthetic base + delocalizeLinks; URL sources are a separate, untouched path.)

FEASIBILITY: src/http/url.ts already provides sameOrigin(a,b) and urlToOutputPath(url,outputDir) (maps a URL to its .md output path from u.pathname, handling trailing-slash→index.md, .html→.md). A new step can map each same-origin link target → output relpath, compute the path relative to the current page's output relpath, preserve the #fragment, then let the existing rewriteInternalLinks/toObsidianWikilinks finish (→ .md / [[wikilink]]). This mirrors delocalizeLinks but keyed on the real origin instead of the docforge.invalid sentinel.
design: New fn (URL analog of delocalizeLinks), e.g. relativizeSameOriginLinks(md, pageUrl, outputDir) in src/http/url.ts or src/links.ts: for each markdown inline-link/autolink with an absolute http(s) target, if sameOrigin(target, pageUrl) → relpath = posix.relative(dirname(relative(outputDir, urlToOutputPath(pageUrl,outputDir))), relative(outputDir, urlToOutputPath(targetNoHash,outputDir))) + originalFragment; else leave untouched (external). Wire into runPipeline HTML branch for URL sources only (item.srcUri is http(s)), before the format rewrite — sibling to the existing delocalizeLinks call for local sources. Emits .md relative links → default keeps them, obsidian → wikilinks (anchor dropped). Reuse existing sameOrigin + urlToOutputPath; do not touch the local/sentinel path.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-page URL crawl (http stub, ≥2 same-origin pages cross-linking, incl. a #fragment link + an external link): --format default emits relative .md links between converted pages (no absolute same-origin https, external links untouched); --format obsidian emits [[wikilinks]] for those same-origin links (anchor dropped) and leaves external links as standard md. about:blank/local-dir behavior (docf-7w5) unchanged. Regression test via existing tests/mcp/helpers/http-stub.ts or tests/http fixtures.
notes: Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-relativize-url-crawl-internal-links.md. Mirrors docf-7w5 delocalizeLinks but for real origin: new relativizeSameOriginLinks(md,pageUrl) in src/http/url.ts (reuses existing sameOrigin + urlToOutputPath; page-vs-asset guard; image-exclude), wired in runPipeline HTML branch for URL sources. 3 TDD tasks.

title: Internal links with #fragment corrupted to about:blank/... on local-dir sources
description:
Relative internal links that carry a #fragment anchor are corrupted to 'about:blank/...' when converting a LOCAL DIRECTORY source (no base URL). Affects BOTH --format default and --format obsidian; obsidian compounds it into invalid wikilinks.

ROOT CAUSE (chain):
- runPipeline.ts:187-189 sets convertOpts.url ONLY for http(s):// sources; local-dir files (item.srcUri = filesystem path) get no base URL.
- convert.ts:60 forwards url to extract only when set.
- extract.ts:30-37 calls Defuddle(document, opts.url ?? '', opts). With local files the base is '' so Defuddle absolutizes relative hrefs that contain a #fragment against an empty/about:blank base, emitting 'about:blank/<path>' AND collapsing the original '../' segments to site-root-relative.
- links.ts rewriteInternalLinks (default) and obsidian.ts toObsidianWikilinks (obsidian) then process the already-corrupted target.

OBSERVED (live verify, 2026-05-24):
- Same-dir link 'b.html#section-two' -> default: '[to B sec](about:blank/b.md#section-two)'; obsidian: '[[c/about:blank/b|...]]'.
- Same-dir link 'b.html' (NO anchor) -> clean 'b.md' / '[[b]]'. Fragment is the sole trigger.
- Cross-dir 'guide/intro.html' link '../api/reference.html#post-widgets' -> obsidian '[[guide/about:blank/api/reference|API reference]]' (invalid; double prefix).
- Anchor-free cross-dir link resolved correctly: '[[guide/intro|intro guide]]'.
- URL sources are NOT affected (real base URL); confirmed via MCP handler against an HTTP stub.

IMPACT: Sphinx/MkDocs corpora (e.g. diadok) use #anchor cross-section links heavily; obsidian output produces broken [[wikilinks]] and default output produces broken relative links for those.

NOT a regression from the obsidian branch: links.ts/convert.ts are unchanged on docf-jee-obsidian-format; the about:blank prefix appears in --format default too.
design: Open design question (settle before plan): (A) give Defuddle a synthetic per-file base so links resolve+re-relativize consistently; (B) strip the 'about:blank/' artifact at a shared normalization point before the format branch — note this yields SITE-ROOT-relative paths, which is exactly correct for obsidian vault-relative wikilinks but changes default's file-relative semantics; (C) suppress Defuddle href absolutization via a defuddle option (needs API check). Fix must repair BOTH formats and be covered by a blockquote/cross-dir + anchored fixture.
acceptance_criteria: Local-dir convert of a cross-directory corpus with #fragment internal links: --format default emits correct internal .md links (no about:blank); --format obsidian emits correct vault-relative [[wikilinks]] with anchor dropped (no about:blank, no double dir prefix). URL-source behavior unchanged. Regression test fixture added covering same-dir + cross-dir anchored links in both formats.
close_reason: Implemented + reviewed (spec+quality+holistic) + live-verified (beads-site obsidian crawl: 13 wikilinks, 0 same-origin absolute body links). Code on branch docf-cf1-relativize-url-links (stacks 7w5+cf1); kept unmerged per user.

title: Add --format obsidian output mode (frontmatter + wikilinks)
description: Add an Obsidian-flavoured Markdown output mode selected via --format obsidian (CLI + MCP convert). Provenance moves to YAML frontmatter (title, source); internal links become vault-relative [[wikilinks]] with anchors dropped. Default format unchanged. Scope: HTML convert + markdown/llms-full passthrough. Out: openapi, callouts, image embeds, related-notes. Design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-obsidian-format-output-design.md
design: Approach A: format flag + branch at runPipeline render step, new src/obsidian.ts (buildObsidianOutput + toObsidianWikilinks). No Renderer abstraction (only 2 formats). Backward compatible: default=current path.
acceptance_criteria: --format obsidian emits YAML frontmatter (title+source) and [[wikilinks]] for internal links; anchors dropped; images/external links untouched; no-flag output byte-identical to current (goldens green); unit tests in tests/obsidian.test.ts; MCP convert accepts format arg.
close_reason: --format obsidian shipped on branch docf-jee-obsidian-format (8 commits, not yet merged to master — user finishing manually). Frontmatter (title+source) + vault [[wikilinks]], CLI --format + MCP convert format arg, default output byte-identical. 399 tests pass, tsc clean, final review = ready to merge. Follow-up docf-apa (readTitle quote-escaping, display-only).

title: Live-test VLM image description against real LM Studio endpoint
description: The VLM image-description pass (docf-i17, shipped) is covered only by unit/integration tests that use a fake node:http VLM server returning canned content. It has never run end-to-end against a real OpenAI-compatible VLM. Validate it against LM Studio on the Windows host (192.168.1.114:1234, OpenAI-compatible, tokenMode: required) with a VISION-capable model loaded (e.g. Qwen2.5-VL) — NOT the canonical qwen3-embedding-8b, which is embedding-only and cannot describe images (README prerequisite). Run 'docforge convert <real-doc-site-url> --output ./md --describe-images --vlm-base-url http://192.168.1.114:1234/v1 --vlm-model <vision-model-id> --vlm-api-key $LMSTUDIO_TOKEN' against a real documentation site that has informative diagrams/screenshots, and inspect the injected '> **Figure — ...**' caption blocks for quality (factual description + verbatim text transcription). Also exercise the MCP convert tool path with DOCFORGE_VLM_* server env set.
acceptance_criteria: Real LM Studio VLM (vision model) reachable + authed from docforge; convert run on a real doc site injects coherent, factual caption blocks after informative images; decorative/tiny images skipped; end-of-run tally (described/skipped/failed/cached) is sane; a second run hits the disk cache (cached>0, described drops); a deliberately-wrong endpoint/model-down skips images + warns + still produces the doc (failure-safe); MCP convert with describe_images=true + DOCFORGE_VLM_* env produces the same captions. Note any prompt/caption-quality tuning needed as follow-up.

title: Strip [#anchor] heading suffixes from llms-full markdown
description: llms-full.txt sources (e.g. lmstudio-dev) embed heading anchors as literal trailing text: 'LM Studio 0.4.1 [#lm-studio-041]'. 611 hits in one file. Pure embedding noise. llms-full passthrough in runPipeline.ts only runs rewriteInternalLinks, no cleaning. Add stripHeadingAnchors to links.ts, apply in the llms-full/markdown branch. Scope confirmed with user: anchors only, leave links as-is.
close_reason: stripHeadingAnchors added to links.ts, applied in runPipeline llms-full branch; 8 tests, full suite 347/347 green

title: HTTP cache is not auth-aware: cached auth-gated responses can be served to unauthenticated fetches
description: The got HTTP/ETag cache in src/http/fetch.ts keys cached responses by URL only. With --cache enabled (CLI) or a non-null cacheDir, a 200 response fetched WITH an Authorization header (docf-eih feature) gets stored under the bare URL key. A later fetch of the same URL WITHOUT auth, pointed at the same cache dir, can be served that cached auth-gated content. Before docf-eih, auth-gated content could not be fetched at all, so docf-eih introduces this exposure. The docf-eih spec deliberately left the cache untouched but did not consider cross-auth-state contamination. Options: disable caching when fetchOptions.auth is set, or namespace the cache dir / cache key per auth-state. Found during code review of docf-eih Task 2.
notes: Reviewer note (docf-eih Task 2 code-quality review): cached responses are stored without auth-awareness; a 200 fetched with auth could be served to a later unauthenticated fetch of the same URL within the same cache directory.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add HTTP auth (username/password or Authorization header) to docforge fetch
description: docforge cannot crawl auth-protected documentation sites. Add ability to pass HTTP Basic credentials (username/password) or a raw Authorization header value, threaded through FetchOptions to fetchUrl. Covers CLI convert + MCP convert tools. Brainstorm -> spec -> plan -> implement.
close_reason: HTTP auth (Authorization header) added to CLI --auth-header + MCP auth_header on convert, origin-scoped via FetchOptions.auth; 401/403 hint, full test coverage, final review passed. Follow-ups: docf-plx (cache), sitemap auth gap.

title: Auto-process OpenAPI specs encountered during HTML crawl
description: HttpSource currently drops non-HTML responses (src/source.ts:73,171,228,277). When a Sphinx/Redoc site links to openapi.json or swagger.yaml, the fetch lands but content-type check skips it, so endpoint+schema fan-out never happens. Detect application/(json|yaml|x-yaml) + path ending .json/.yaml/.yml, peek bytes for 'openapi:' or 'swagger:' top-level key, mark SourceItem kind='openapi', route through src/openapi/pipeline.ts in runPipeline.ts.
close_reason: Added isOpenapiCandidate + tryParseOpenapiSpec in src/source.ts; route SourceItem.kind='openapi' through runOpenapiPipeline in runPipeline.ts. Detection: ctype application/(json|yaml|x-yaml|vnd.oai.openapi[+json|+yaml]) OR text/(yaml|x-yaml) OR path .json/.yaml/.yml ext; peek must parse + have 'openapi':'3.x'. Swagger 2.0 and non-3.x rejected. 9 tests added across source-openapi.test.ts + pipeline-openapi.test.ts. Full suite 323/323.

title: Fix URL percent-encoding collision in crawler
description: normalizeUrl() in src/http/url.ts treats '/820@/Item820' and '/820%40/Item820' as distinct URLs. Both pass sameOrigin host check, both get fetched, both map to same output path via urlToOutputPath -> write collision aborts crawl. Real-world hit: developer.kontur.ru Sphinx site with cross-links using mixed @ and %40 encoding. Canonicalize percent-encoding in normalizeUrl by decoding then re-encoding pathname segments.
close_reason: Fixed via canonicalizePercentEncoding in src/http/url.ts. Decodes safe path-segment chars (RFC 3986 unreserved + sub-delims + : @) and uppercases hex for non-decodable bytes. 6 new tests in tests/http-url.test.ts cover %40/@ collision, %7E decode, %2F preserve, hex case normalization, malformed encoding tolerance, output-path collision.

title: docforge: 4-way A/B add raw-no-enrich arm
description: Run 4-way retrieval A/B baseline/nogloss/tight/raw vs gold to decompose translation contribution from multilingual vec alone. Setup: ingest ~/docs/diadok-md (raw) as new qmd collection diadok-md-raw-642 then score all 4 against 15-query gold. Caveat: 15-query gold post-hoc biased; rigorous follow-up = docf-73t.
notes:
4-way gold A/B (15 queries, post-hoc gold). RUN 2026-05-11.

Collections scored:
- baseline (qmd diadok-md → ~/docs/diadok-md-enriched, v1 glossary+EN)
- nogloss  (diadok-md-noglossary-642, v2 EN summary, schema v2)
- tight    (diadok-md-tight-642, terse EN no-enumeration)
- raw      (diadok-md-raw-642, ~/docs/diadok-md, NO enrichment, RU body only)

Results:
collection   top1@gold  top3@gold  MRR    miss
baseline     10/15      12/15      0.767  1
nogloss      12/15      15/15      0.900  0
tight         9/15      10/15      0.683  1
raw          12/15      14/15      0.880  0

KEY FINDING: raw (no enrichment, pure RU + Qwen3-Embedding-8B-Q4 multilingual vec) TIES nogloss on top1@gold. Translation contribution = +1 top3 + 0.020 MRR. Within 15-query noise floor.

Surprise: Q4+Q12 (signer details with INN) — only raw scores top1. RU body "ИНН" repeats outweigh single EN mention; multilingual vec bridges cross-lingual gap.

Q9 (deal documentation flow) — only EN-enriched arms win. So EN summary DOES help some queries.

Q7 (electronic signature) — flipped vs prior 3-way run (baseline+raw G, nogloss+tight X). Reranker + LLM-expansion non-determinism. Same gold definition.

CAVEATS:
- 15 queries, post-hoc, single observer (confirmation-bias risk)
- All queries EN; RU-query mix likely widens raw's lead
- Reranker variance ~1 query flip between runs

INTERPRETATION:
- Multilingual vec handles cross-lingual retrieval respectably on its own
- qmd BM25 layer benefits from EN tokens but RU body already has rich technical-term overlap (URLs, code identifiers, schema names) — diminishing return on translation
- Translation gain is real but small; not statistically defensible on 15 queries
- Need docf-73t (rigorous gold) to settle

NEXT: docf-73t — multi-doc hand-authored gold, blind authoring, larger N (50+), include RU-language queries.
close_reason: 4-way A/B complete. Raw arm proves multilingual vec handles RU→EN solo respectably; v2 enrich gain ~marginal on 15-query gold. Results captured in notes + memory docf-mt4-4-way-a-b-2026-05. Rigorous settlement awaits docf-73t.

title: enrich: drop glossary field from EnrichmentResponse, ship schema v2 (no-glossary, bilingual-summary-only)
description:
## Why

Pending validation by the full-corpus A/B experiment. If experiment confirms no-regression, glossary is removable: inline bilingual term mentions in summary cover the same retrieval surface with less hallucination, less LLM output cost, and zero glossary-direction inconsistency (docf-5oj).

## What

Schema change:

\`\`\`typescript
// src/enrich/types.ts
export interface EnrichmentResponse {
  title_en: string;
  summary_en: string;
  // glossary: REMOVED in v2
}

export const ENRICH_JSON_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['title_en', 'summary_en'],
  properties: {
    title_en: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 },
    summary_en: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 },
  },
};

export const ENRICH_SCHEMA_VERSION = 2;
\`\`\`

Prompt change (src/enrich/prompt.ts):

- Drop rule 3 (glossary)
- Strengthen rule 2 (summary): \"3-4 sentences. CRITICAL: mention each major Russian acronym, regulatory ID, and key technical term ONCE on first occurrence with its English equivalent in parentheses, e.g. 'ТОРГ-12 (goods consignment note)', 'ЭДО (electronic document interchange / EDI)'. This serves as the only English-Russian bridge — no separate glossary will be emitted.\"
- Drop seed-glossary injection (no longer needed)
- Drop aggregator (GlossaryAggregator becomes dead code, remove)

Output change (src/enrich/output.ts):

- Drop \`glossary\` from frontmatter
- Drop \`> Keys:\` line from blockquote (only \`> EN:\` remains)
- Bump \`enrich_schema_version\` to 2

Pipeline change (src/enrich/pipeline.ts):

- Remove GlossaryAggregator, glossary seed loading, aggregator.absorb calls
- Cache invalidation: schema_version bump → all v1 cache entries become invalid (already keyed on enrich_hash which incorporates schema_version)

CLI change:

- Drop \`--glossary-seed\` flag from enrich subcommand
- Update --help

Tests:

- Update llm-client.test.ts: remove glossary assertions
- Update pipeline.test.ts (if any glossary path exists): remove
- Update output.test.ts: assert no \`glossary:\` in frontmatter, no \`> Keys:\` line
- Update eval/runner.test.ts: glossary metric becomes vestigial — either drop from runner or keep as legacy/zero
- Update gold fixture (tests/enrich/fixtures/gold/reference/torg12.json): remove glossary field
- Add new golden output fixtures for v2 schema

Migration:

- Existing diadok-md-enriched dir is v1. Re-run \`docforge enrich --force\` to produce v2.
- Old enrich_hash cache entries invalidated automatically (schema_version is in hash input)
- Document migration in CHANGELOG (if exists) or README

## Closes / supersedes

- docf-5oj (glossary direction inconsistency) — moot when glossary removed
- Any future docf-* glossary-related bugs

## Depends on

- Decision experiment bead (full-corpus A/B). Don't ship until that bead closes with positive result.
close_reason: closed by docf-eew commit 993ea31 (schema v2 ships; glossary removed)

title: enrich: inject per-section preamble for big docs to lift chunk-level EN retrieval
description:
## Why

qmd embeds each chunk as \`title + chunk_text\` (verified: store.ts:1524 + llm.ts:51). Only doc TITLE is prepended; summary_en + glossary live ONLY in YAML frontmatter at top of file → only chunk 0 carries them.

Big docs (utd.md = 82K → ~30 chunks) end up with 1 EN-anchored chunk + 29 bare-Russian chunks. EN semantic queries match the title-only signal weakly → big docs surface but lose to atomic schema docs (e.g. Q11 \"parse buyer title received document\" → utd.md ranked #2 @ 56% vs ParseUniversalCorrectionDocumentBuyerTitleXml.md @ 93%).

qmd has no chunk-prefix injection knob. \`addContext\` is display-only (returned in search-result \`context\` field, never embedded — checked store.ts:3573/3718 + embed loop at 1524).

## qmd chunking algorithm (per upstream README)

- Target ~900 tokens, scan 200-token window before cutoff
- Break-point scores: H1=100, H2=90, H3=80, H4=70, code-fence=80, hr=60, blank=20, list=5
- Decay: \`score × (1 - (distance/window)² × 0.7)\` — squared distance penalty
- Code fences NOT cut points (kept whole)
- Markdown uses regex chunking only (AST chunking is .ts/.js/.py/.go/.rs only)

Implication: qmd cuts AT \`## \` boundaries (score 90, dominates). Each chunk STARTS with \`## Section\` heading.

## What

Post-enrich pass over enriched .md output:
1. Detect docs above threshold (proposal: > 4K tokens, ~50 of 642 diadok docs)
2. Parse frontmatter (title_en, summary_en, glossary)
3. Walk markdown; **immediately AFTER each \`## \` and \`### \` heading**, inject a compact preamble block:
   \`\`\`
   ## Section Title
   
   <!-- doc-anchor -->
   > **Doc:** {title_en}. {summary_en truncated ~200 chars}
   > **Keys:** k1=v1; k2=v2; ... (top-N glossary entries by source-frequency)
   
   [section body]
   \`\`\`
4. Skip H4+ (anchor spam)
5. Emit rewritten .md to same output path (overwrite)

## Placement rationale

qmd cuts AT the H2 boundary → anchor MUST go AFTER the heading so it lands at the START of the new chunk (not end of previous).

WRONG: \`> anchor\n## Section\` — anchor stays in prior chunk.
RIGHT: \`## Section\n> anchor\n[body]\` — anchor enters new chunk's first ~150 tokens.

## Acceptance

- Module \`src/enrich/section-anchor.ts\` + tests
- CLI flag \`--anchor-sections\` on \`docforge enrich\` (off by default; opt-in until validated)
- Unit test: utd-shaped fixture with 5 H2 + 3 H3 sections → output has 8 anchor blocks each placed AFTER the corresponding heading
- Unit test: small doc (< threshold) → no rewrite, byte-identical
- Unit test: doc with H4+ headings → H4+ skipped, no anchor injected
- A/B retrieval smoke: rerun 8-query smoke before/after \`--anchor-sections\` flag — utd.md should rank ≥ #1 on at least 1 of (Q9 seller title, Q10 amendment, Q11 parse buyer title)
- Threshold + top-N glossary truncation are config flags

## Open

- Glossary truncation strategy: top-N by source frequency vs all (latter inflates chunks). Start with N=8.
- Anchor format: blockquote (\`> Doc:\`) vs HTML comment (\`<!-- Doc: ... -->\`) — blockquote IS visible in rendered MD but doesn't disrupt downstream tools. Comment is invisible but qmd may strip in future. Lean blockquote.
- Mid-section overflow chunks (sections > 900 tokens split into 2+ chunks): chunks beyond first lose anchor. Accepted trade-off — most diadok sections are <900 tokens. Revisit if measurable.

title: enrich: investigate context-overflow on small input docs
description: 11 docs failed with HTTP 400 'Context size has been exceeded' on diadok run 2026-05-10 even at 32K LM Studio context. Most are huge inputs (ReleaseNotes 268K, GetContent 263K) but orderrequest.md is only 6.4K (~2K tokens). Hypothesis: JSON schema strict mode (llama.cpp grammar) causes unbounded generation loop on certain Russian formal-doc patterns. Repro: run enrich on diadok-md-fresh corpus with qwen3.5-9b @ 32768 context. Fix candidates: (a) add max_tokens cap on completion request, (b) limit glossary to N keys via schema.maxProperties, (c) bump LM Studio to 96K context for outliers, (d) add --max-bytes flag to enrich CLI to skip huge inputs.
close_reason: Root cause: docforge omitted max_tokens, causing llama.cpp to reserve prompt+remaining_context as n_keep. Under parallel sharding (n_ctx/parallel), reservation overflowed even for small inputs. Fix in commit on master adds max_tokens=2048. Live diadok run: 8/8 Type-B failures resolved at parallel=1 even before fix; with fix, parallel=4 should also work. 3 Type-A failures (true input-overflow on huge docs) need either 96K context or smart truncation -- separate issue if pursued.

title: [docforge] test kreuzberg-py binding vs html-to-markdown lib
description:
Mirror the docforge_node v0.2 experiment in Python: swap docforge's html_to_markdown.convert call for kreuzberg.extract_bytes_sync with output_format='markdown'. Keep BS4 preprocessing (body-picker + noise-strip + Pygments flatten). Run on diadok 642-file corpus, diff body-MD vs current docforge output.

Hypothesis: Same 98.3% parity + perf delta as Node port. If true, docforge could drop html-to-markdown dep (no longer maintained as separate kreuzberg-dev release? — verify) and stand on the kreuzberg core directly, with same plugin/eval ecosystem access as the rest of kreuzberg.

Acceptance:
- new branch + venv install kreuzberg python package
- swap convert.py:_convert call to kreuzberg.extract_bytes_sync(cleaned_body_html, 'text/html', config=ExtractionConfig(output_format='markdown'))
- run pytest tests/ — record golden delta
- run convert against ~/docs/diadok, diff body-MD vs current /tmp/diadok-py output
- report: % match, perf, dep size

Reference: bd memory docforge-node-v0-2-diadok-dogfood for the Node version's findings + the outputFormat='markdown' gotcha (default is Plain — drops headings/lists/fences).
close_reason: kreuzberg engine swap landed. Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-docforge-kreuzberg-rewrite-design.md; plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-09-docforge-kreuzberg-rewrite.md. Goldens regenerated (only sphinx-method diffed; 5 of 6 already match Kreuzberg output). Full corpus 642/642, --version stamps 0.3.0, all 8 acceptance gates passed (incl. uv tool install reinstalled local docforge to 0.3.0).

Diff against Python docforge output on diadok corpus afterward.

Quality lower than Python docforge — loses H1, loses list dashes, duplicates nested content. 1 fixture (sphinx-method) hits upstream kreuzberg-go schema bug (DjotLink.Attributes *string, Rust emits array). Decision: keep Python docforge as primary, docforge_go as POC/reference.

See ~/experiements/docforge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-docforge-design.md §0,9,10,11.
close_reason: Closed

title: docforge: optional inline-link → reference-style citation pass for HTML→MD (crawl4ai technique)
description: Port crawl4ai's markdown_with_citations/references_markdown idea (DefaultMarkdownGenerator options={citations:True}). Convert inline [text](url) in the converted HTML body to anchor-text + numbered marker, collecting URLs into a trailing references block, to cut inline-URL embedding noise for RAG. Gated by a new CLI flag (default OFF). Implement as a post-processing pass in runPipeline.ts AFTER link rewrite and BEFORE frontmatter wrap; reuse the edit-from-end replacement pattern of rewriteImageRefs (assets/core.ts:16).
design: MEASURED value ceiling (verify-before-assert, 2026-05-25 session). qmd embeds chunk text verbatim incl URLs (qmd llm.ts:116 formatDocForEmbedding). qmd chunker is structure-aware and a doc <=3600 chars becomes ONE whole-doc chunk (qmd store.ts:283). Therefore: (1) short single-chunk docs gain NOTHING — URL stays in the same chunk; (2) only long, link-dense docs benefit, as URLs move out of body chunks into the trailing references chunk; (3) Obsidian: convert EXTERNAL http(s) links only — leave internal [[wikilinks]] (obsidian.ts) untouched or vault navigation breaks. Value is MODEST/conditional; do not overstate.
acceptance_criteria: Flag default OFF; obsidian mode converts external links only; internal links/wikilinks unchanged; single trailing references block; existing goldens byte-identical when flag OFF.
notes: Sibling crawl4ai HTML->MD techniques evaluated and REJECTED this session: (a) body_width=0 no-wrap — docforge already emits unwrapped MD; kreuzberg-node runtime default is wrap=OFF (measured: default maxLine=341 == wrap:false; .d.ts 'Default: true' is stale). No-op, not filed. (b) PruningContentFilter — overlaps Defuddle; only its link_density metric is marginally useful (separate measure-first task if Sphinx/TOC sidebars leak). (c) BM25 query filter — wrong fit for whole-corpus ingest. (d) LLMContentFilter — cost-prohibitive/nondeterministic for batch. (e) crawl4ai chunkers (TextTiling/overlapping-window) — all plain-text; qmd's heading-scored chunker is strictly better. crawl4ai source refs: markdown_generation_strategy.py, content_filter_strategy.py @ unclecode/crawl4ai main.

title: MCP readTitle drops titles containing double-quotes
description: convert.ts readTitle() regex /^title:\s*"?([^"\n]+?)"?\s*$/m stops at the first embedded double-quote, so an Obsidian frontmatter title like title: "He said \"hi\"" fails to parse and readTitle returns "". The MCP convert structured response then falls back to rel_path for that page's title (convert.ts:346). Written .md files are unaffected — display-only, MCP response title field only. Pre-existing; newly observable because --format obsidian is the first output mode that writes a frontmatter title: line (default format uses an inline Source: line). Common case (title without embedded quotes) parses fine. Fix: match quoted value with escapes ("((?:[^"\\]|\\.)*)") and unescape \" and \\, keeping the unquoted-title branch. Found in docf-jee final review.

title: [docforge] VLM image description pass (URL sources, v1)
description: Opt-in post-Markdown enrichment pass: for crawled HTML (URL) sources, find informative raster image refs in the converted MD, fetch each via existing fetchUrl (cache+auth), send to an OpenAI-compatible VLM (LM Studio, e.g. Qwen2.5-VL) for a factual description+text-transcription, and inject a delimited caption block after the image. Recovers content that today survives only as weak alt-text and is lost to qmd RAG. Lives outside the deterministic core so base-conversion goldens stay byte-stable; failure-safe (model down -> skip+warn, never fails the doc); cached by sha256(bytes)+model+promptVersion. Capability-push: user has RTX 5090 + LM Studio. Output format: caption block after image (chosen in brainstorming). Scope v1: URL sources only (local-file image resolution deferred). SVG deferred. See spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-docforge-vlm-image-description-design.md.
design: Hook in runPipeline.ts:190-196 on bodyMd after rewriteInternalLinks, before buildOutput, gated on http(s) srcUri + opt-in flag. New module src/vlm/. Reuse opts.fetchOptions for image fetch. Config via CLI flags + env (DOCFORGE_VLM_BASE_URL/MODEL/API_KEY) + MCP convert args. Mock client for unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: With --describe-images off, output byte-identical to today (goldens unchanged). With it on against a URL source: informative raster images get a '> **Figure ...**' caption block injected after the ref; tiny/decorative/icon images skipped; model outage/timeout skips image + warns + doc still converts; repeated identical image described once (cache hit); end-of-run tally reports described/skipped/failed.
close_reason: VLM image description pass shipped (URL sources): select -> fetch -> describe -> caption, opt-in, cached, failure-safe; CLI + MCP surfaces; 9 tasks, two-stage reviewed; 380 tests green

title: Sitemap discovery does not forward Authorization header on auth-gated sites
description: discoverSitemaps/fetchSitemap in src/http/sitemap.ts construct a fresh Sitemapper instance with its own requestHeaders ({user-agent} only) instead of going through fetchUrl, so FetchOptions.auth is never applied. On a site that gates everything behind auth, /sitemap.xml and robots.txt-declared sitemap URLs are fetched unauthenticated and return 401, so discoverSitemaps returns [] and HttpSource.iter falls back to iterFromBfs (link-following) instead of iterFromSitemap. Graceful degradation (no crash, no header leak) but a real coverage gap: an authed crawl of a sitemap-driven site silently loses sitemap coverage. Fix: in fetchSitemap, add requestHeaders.authorization when opts.auth && new URL(url).origin === opts.auth.origin, mirroring the fetchUrl origin gate. Also: the docf-eih design spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-docforge-http-auth-design.md) claims auth 'threads through automatically' to all fetch sites — that is inaccurate for the sitemap path (Sitemapper is a separate HTTP client) and should be corrected. Found during final code review of docf-eih.
notes: Found in docf-eih final holistic review. Related: docf-eih (the HTTP auth feature), docf-plx (cache not auth-aware). Low urgency — BFS fallback keeps authed crawls functional.
close_reason: Closed

title: enrich: register per-file EN context with qmd via 'qmd context add' for search-result UX
description:
## Why

qmd's \`addContext(collectionName, pathPrefix, contextText)\` API stores per-path context that is RETURNED in search results (in the \`context\` field of get/multi-get/query results — verified store.ts:3573/3718/4193/4267).

This context is NOT embedded into vectors (so it does NOT help retrieval — that's owned by docf-1fi's anchor injection). But it IS surfaced to AI consumers reading qmd search output, so it improves the *interpretability* of every hit.

Currently the consumer only sees: filepath, title, body snippet (in Russian). Adding EN context means every hit ships with an EN summary the consumer can reason about without parsing frontmatter from the body.

## What

After \`docforge enrich\` completes, walk the enriched output dir and call qmd CLI to register per-file context:

\`\`\`
qmd context add diadok-md <relpath> "{title_en}. {summary_en}\nKeys: k1=v1; k2=v2; ..."
\`\`\`

Or programmatically via qmd's \`addContext\` API (if we depend on qmd as a library).

## Acceptance

- New CLI flag on \`docforge enrich\`: \`--register-qmd-context <collection>\` (off by default)
- When set, post-enrich step shells out to \`qmd context add <collection> <relpath> "<text>"\` for each enriched file
- Context text format: \`{title_en}. {summary_en truncated to 300 chars}. Keys: {top-8 glossary entries}\`
- Skip files where context already matches (idempotent)
- Smoke: enrich gold fixture with flag, run \`qmd context list <collection>\` → entry visible
- Smoke: \`qmd query "consignment note" --collection diadok-md\` → result \`context\` field contains the registered EN summary

## Notes

- Cheap (~30 LOC + tests). Independent of docf-1fi.
- Stacks well with docf-1fi: anchor injection lifts retrieval (vector-side); context add lifts result interpretability (display-side).
- Failure mode: if qmd CLI not on PATH, log warning, skip. Don't fail the enrich run.

title: enrich: model produces reversed/identity glossary entries (EN as key, RU as value)
description: Aggregated glossary from diadok run 2026-05-10 has 2079 entries with quality issues: (1) some keys are EN with RU value (e.g. '1C module: 1С модуль', 'acceptance certificate: акт о выполнении работ / оказании услуг'); (2) some are identity (e.g. '200 OK: 200 OK', '401 Unauthorized: 401 Unauthorized'); (3) some are definitional not translational (e.g. '402 Payment Required: API subscription expired for the specified box'). Prompt should enforce: key MUST be Russian (Cyrillic) source term; value MUST be English translation; skip identity mappings; never include error-code descriptions. Fix: tighten BASE_SYSTEM in src/enrich/prompt.ts with explicit examples of allowed/disallowed glossary entries. Add a JSON schema constraint or post-validator that drops entries where the key is pure ASCII.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-11: glossary direction bug may be moot if docf-slw confirms no-glossary is viable. If docf-slw → docf-eew (drop glossary), this issue becomes superseded. Revisit only if docf-eew is rejected.
close_reason: closed by docf-eew commit 993ea31 (schema v2 ships; glossary removed)

title: Rebuild docforge enrich gold corpus with multi-doc hand-authored pairs
description: Current gold has 1 stub fixture that was hand-fixed during smoke. For real eval coverage need 5-10 hand-authored RU source/reference pairs spanning protocol docs, REST endpoints, and proto messages. Each reference should reflect realistic enrich output shape (RU->EN glossary, summary mentioning Diadoc-specific terms, must_preserve only listing tokens present in source).

title: enrich: warn on empty-string env var interpolation in config
description:
src/enrich/config.ts:96 interpolateEnv replaces unset vars with ''. For api_key that may be intentional (no auth), but for endpoint/model an unset var silently produces broken values like 'http:///v1'. Add a warn log when interpolation yields empty for required fields.
Source: final code review of d4916e8

title: enrich: extract expandHome to shared util + remove unused buildUserMessage
description:
Final review of M6 found two HIGH cleanup items:
1) expandHome duplicated across 4 files (src/cli.ts uses process.env.HOME chain, src/enrich/cli.ts + src/enrich/eval/cli.ts + src/openapi/cli.ts use os.homedir()). Extract to src/utils/path.ts.
2) buildUserMessage in src/enrich/prompt.ts exported but never called by pipeline/cli/runner. Either wire it through callLlm userMessage construction or remove.
Source: final code review of d4916e8

title: [docforge] Structured JSON report on stdout
description: Add --report-json flag. Emit {converted, failed, skipped, files: [...]} on stdout, logs still on stderr. Trigger: piping into CI/reporting tools.

title: [docforge] Incremental conversion (mtime/hash skip)
description: Skip files where output mtime is newer than input. Optional content hash check. Trigger: corpora grow large, full reconvert too slow.

title: [docforge] Configurable enrichment story
description: Per-source rules to add tags/category/version/lang to output frontmatter or Source-line tags. .docforge.yml in source root. Trigger: downstream consumer needs these fields.

title: [docforge] Plugin registry seam (Option C)
description: Define backend interface, ship Kreuzberg+Docling+native by default. Anyone adds backend via entry-point. Trigger: 2nd backend competes for same format (e.g. MinerU vs Docling for PDFs).

title: [docforge] Add concurrency --jobs N
description: Parallel file conversion via multiprocessing or threading. Only needed if real corpus exceeds 10s single-threaded. Trigger: corpus growing past 5000 files.

title: [docforge] Add --selector CLI override + broader selector chain
description: --selector flag accepts CSS selector. Default chain extends with main, article, .content. Handles MkDocs/Hugo/generic HTML. Trigger: non-Sphinx custom themes.

title: [docforge] Add URL fetch input
description: Support docforge https://docs.foo.com/page --output ./out. Single page in v2, recursive crawl deferred. Add httpx dep. Trigger: downloading docs from web routinely.

title: [docforge] Add OpenAPI/Swagger adapter
description: Parse OpenAPI YAML/JSON, emit one MD per endpoint with method, path, params, responses. Trigger: ingesting API spec.
close_reason: Shipped in docforge 0.2.0. `docforge openapi <spec> --output <dir>` emits per-endpoint + per-schema MD with relative $ref links. Validated end-to-end against diadoc spec (125 endpoints + 483 schemas in <1s, qmd ingest + cross-lingual smoke query passing).

title: [docforge] Add MD passthrough adapter
description: Adapter for existing MD trees: read file, prepend # title + Source line, write to output. No conversion. Trigger: ingesting existing MD docs (READMEs, MkDocs source).
close_reason: Marginal value. qmd ingests MD natively; header rewrite mostly cosmetic since qmd metadata already exposes path. Reopen if reranker proves to need inline path provenance.

title: [docforge] Add code-repo walker adapter
description: Walk git tree, classify files by extension, concat with breadcrumbs. Output one MD per file or one MD per dir. Trigger: ingesting code repo as searchable docs.
close_reason: Superseded by qmd --chunk-strategy auto (native AST chunking for code files). Walking repo → MD wrappers loses AST signal vs qmd ingesting source directly with glob mask.

title: [docforge] Add Office (DOCX/PPTX/XLSX) backend via Docling
description: Add Docling backend for Office formats. Router dispatches DOCX/PPTX/XLSX to Docling, HTML stays on Kreuzberg. Trigger: first time user needs to ingest Word/PowerPoint.

title: enrich: handle 'skipped' status in CLI tally + threshold denominator
description:
src/enrich/types.ts EnrichStatus declares 'skipped' but src/enrich/cli.ts only counts ok/cached/failed. If 'skipped' is ever emitted it drops silently. Either remove from union or add tally + threshold-denominator handling.
Source: final code review of d4916e8
